title: Korean Shrimp Clay Pot Delight
description: A savory and spicy Korean-inspired shrimp dish cooked in a clay pot. This recipe is perfect for a quick and flavorful meal.
instructions: Preheat the clay pot at 400°F (200°C) for 10 minutes.
Rinse the shrimp and pat dry with paper towels.
In a small bowl, mix together chili flakes, soy sauce, brown sugar, and rice vinegar.
Add the garlic and ginger to the bowl and stir to combine.
Add the shrimp to the bowl and toss to coat with the marinade.
Heat 1 tablespoon of sesame oil in the preheated clay pot over medium-high heat.
Add the shrimp to the clay pot and cook for 2-3 minutes per side, until pink and cooked through.
Remove the shrimp from the clay pot and set aside.
In the same clay pot, add the remaining 1 tablespoon of sesame oil.
Add the green onions and sesame seeds to the clay pot and stir-fry until fragrant.
Return the shrimp to the clay pot and toss to combine with the green onions and sesame seeds.
